## Summary

Repair `#817` on top of current `main` so the Story Ledger contract is consistently nine-layer and uses shared canonical layer constants.

- centralize/use `STORY_LAYER_KEYS`
- centralize/use `STORY_LAYER_COUNT`
- align schemas/envelopes/tests with `identity_pronoun_layer`
- remove stale hardcoded 8-layer assumptions

**Touches:**
- `lib/evaluation/artifacts/artifactTypes.ts`
- `lib/evaluation/phase1a/storyLayerArtifactWriters.ts`
- `lib/evaluation/stage-machine/hardStopGuards.ts`
- `schemas/evaluation/pass1a_story_layer_v1.schema.json`

- `schemas/evaluation/ledger_user_feedback_v1.schema.json`

- `__tests__/evaluation/schemas.envelope.test.ts`
- `__tests__/components/ledger/storyLayerMetadata.test.ts`

- `components/ledger/StoryLedgerLayers.tsx`
- `components/ledger/storyLayerMetadata.ts`
- `docs/canon/STORY_LAYER_CONTRACT_V1.md`

**Does NOT touch:**
- `lib/ledger/storyLedgerVisibility.ts`
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/identityNameHygiene.ts`
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/identityReducerFallback.ts`
- `lib/revision/workbenchQueue.ts`
- relationship repair
- timeline/threat repair
- Source Integrity aggregation
- Revise UI work

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: no

- [x] No new evaluation phase, job status, or contract identifier introduced.
- [x] No scoring, prompt, or phase-order semantics changed.
- [x] No visibility/admission semantics changed.
- [x] This PR only repairs nine-layer contract consistency and shared metadata wiring.

## Contract Integrity

- `#823` Story Ledger visibility gate remains server-side and unchanged.
- `#820` canonical identity hygiene and fallback parity remain unchanged.
- `#821` Revise admission synthesis behavior remains unchanged.
- `pass1a_story_layer_v1` remains a canonical nine-layer artifact with `identity_pronoun_layer` included.
- Shared constants (`STORY_LAYER_KEYS`, `STORY_LAYER_COUNT`) are the contract source of truth.

## Behavioral Quality

- Story Ledger UI now reads shared layer metadata instead of duplicated local tables.
- Nine-layer counts and required-field checks come from canonical shared constants.
- Visible-layer review flow from `#823` remains intact.
- This PR does not introduce semantic layer repair; it only removes stale contract drift.

## Risks & Anomalies

- Main risk is accidental scope creep during conflict repair; this was mitigated by taking current `main` as source of truth and reapplying only contract-level hunks.
- Shared metadata centralization touches Story Ledger UI files, so visibility regression tests were run to prove `#823` behavior remains intact.
- Repo-wide TypeScript currently has unrelated baseline issues outside touched files.

No-Pipeline-Impact: true — contract/constants/schema/test repair only; no runtime pipeline expansion.
